title: provider (plugin)
objectives: Wire providers cleanly at the app root
Combine multiple providers with MultiProvider / ProxyProvider

sections.markdown: `provider` is a lightweight wrapper around InheritedWidget with builders for ChangeNotifier, Streams, Futures, and dependent providers. It's the officially recommended baseline for shared state.

Under the hood everything provider offers is an InheritedWidget — the value is that you don't have to write one. It's the fastest way to teach a team dependency injection + state without a heavier framework.
Using `context.watch` inside a callback → rebuild loops / assertion errors. Use `context.read` in callbacks and event handlers; use `watch` / `Consumer` / `Selector` only inside `build`.
For granular rebuilds use `Selector<T, S>` or Consumer's `child:` parameter to avoid rebuilding heavy subtrees. Consider Riverpod when compile-time safety and multi-instance providers matter.

flashcards.back: watch subscribes and rebuilds; read is a one-off access (callbacks/handlers).
ProxyProvider (or ChangeNotifierProxyProvider) — rebuilds when its dependencies change.

quiz.explain: It derives its value from other providers.

challenges.text: Selector to rebuild only one label.
ProxyProvider deriving an Api from an AuthNotifier.
Refactor an over-rebuilding screen; measure rebuild count before/after.

interviewQuestions: How does provider relate to InheritedWidget?
When would you leave provider for Riverpod?
How to test code that depends on provider?
